h2h_summary: Türkiye and Paraguay have met only once in recorded history, a goalless friendly draw in June 1995, making this World Cup encounter their first competitive meeting.
team_style_home: Türkiye line up in a fluid 4-2-3-1 under Vincenzo Montella, blending possession-based build-up with high pressing and relying on the creativity of Güler and Yıldız to unlock defences in the final third.
team_style_away: Paraguay under Gustavo Alfaro deploy a compact 4-4-2 rooted in defensive intensity and collective pressing, building patiently from the back before releasing Almirón and Enciso on the counter-attack.
wc_history_home: Türkiye's finest World Cup moment came in 2002, when they finished third — their only other appearance before 2026 being the 1954 group stage — with Hakan Şükür scoring the fastest goal in World Cup history (10.8 seconds) in the third-place match against South Korea.
wc_history_away: Paraguay have appeared in ten World Cups, with their best run coming in 2010 when they reached the quarter-finals before losing 1-0 to eventual champions Spain, while their 1998 campaign also featured a memorable unbeaten group stage run before a golden-goal defeat to France.
group_context: Group D — the USA, Türkiye, Paraguay, and Australia — is one of the most evenly matched groups of the tournament; the Turkey vs Paraguay clash on matchday 2 is likely a decisive game for second place, with both sides needing points after their respective openers against the USA and Australia.
strengths_home: Elite creative talent in Güler and Yıldız capable of unlocking any defence
Çalhanoğlu's leadership, set-piece delivery and deep-lying playmaking
Strong qualification form — only one loss (to Spain) in final 10 matches
strengths_away: Defensively disciplined 4-4-2 system that concedes very few goals
Almirón and Enciso provide dangerous counter-attacking pace and invention
Gustavo Alfaro's battle-hardened squad psychologically prepared for tournament football
